title: TP-Link Tapo P110M Smart Plug 4-Pack $68 + Delivery ($0 to Metro/ OnePass/ in-Store/ C&C) @ Officeworks
body:
Deal: TP-Link Tapo P110M Smart Plug 4-Pack $68 + Delivery ($0 to Metro/ OnePass/ in-Store/ C&C) @ Officeworks

[rhino86] Boil the jug.
Mini version, was $66 now $38 at jbhi: https://www.jbhifi.com.au/products/tp-link-tapo-mini-smart-p…
[eeeee] I will take half a plug. Bottom half thanks
[3dprintdeals] No power monitoring on the P100.
Don't need it for everything but for some automatons it's useful .
I have one each on the washing machine and dryer to tell me when they are done via home assistant
[maruko] Need to check if they are rated for inductive loads - usually only 10A resistive and so you may be overloading them
[3dprintdeals] It's been there for over a year with no issues
[strazto] Yeah I've heard people say this, and I've seen someone claim they contacted TP-Link and asked support, and support has confirmed they might not be suitable for inductive loads
Personally, I'm a little unsure of what exactly the risk would be with these - when they switch on I can hear a physical "click", so I have to assume there's an actual relay clicking on in there, so besides the power monitoring, I don't really see which component would cause issues with inductive loads
[3dprintdeals] @strazto: I dont actually turn it on and off, it's just used for power monitoring.
[LowRange] @strazto: The relay burns out and no more clicky clicky.
[mandxtista] How do you configure to tell you when the washing machine is done ?
[noz] https://github.com/3dg1luk43/ha_washdata
[frkino] Thanks OP
Important to note, these plugs are Matter-over-WiFi only, instead of Thread
Protocol IEEE 802.11b/g/n, Bluetooth (for setup only)
Wireless Type 2.4 GHz WiFi Only
spec sheet: https://www.tp-link.com/au/home-networking/smart-plug/tapo-p…
[S Baldrick] Anyone using these with Home Assistant? I heard somewhere that TP-Link has been breaking third-party integrations.
[3dprintdeals] Yeah works fine,
It might be the local only extensions
[strazto] You don't need third party integrations for the p110ms, you can control them directly using matter in home assistant, and the matter implementation is now feature complete with what the tapo app exposes as of firmware 1.4 or so
[S Baldrick] Was wondering if they worked fine in Home Assistant, without an internet connection
[Stability] I am running three of these on Matter Server 8.5.0 on HAOS, work fine. But when I upgraded it to 9.0.x, nothing worked. Fortunately I checked the backup button after reading "It is recommended to take a backup before updating." on the release notes. I will wait for a longer while before upgrading it again.

[Zippy7] Looks like your link is for P110, not P110M (which is this post)
In this case the Bunnings deal at $11 each is better for you.
[batrarobin] Isn't this deal better? https://www.ozbargain.com.au/node/965797
[3dprintdeals] Yep, it's for the P110 version, not the P110M.
If it doesn't Matter to you
[Benji] Previously smart devices from different brands may not have worked together. Matter is a way for them to communicate and lets you mix and match across brands any product that supports it.
Also, Matter devices can use Wifi or Threads, and a Wifi and Threads device can't link to each other directly. You need a device that speaks both if you want them to work together (like an Apple Homepod).
Matter is also sometimes more limited. These smart plugs show energy usage, but not if you use them in an Apple Home with Matter.
[cheekymonkey97] What do people use these for considering a lot of devices still need you to go press the on button after giving them power through these.
[ESEMCE] I have mine on the coffee machine, replacing a timer socket.
Now I can wake up to a pre-heated machine on weekdays weekdays and can remote start on Weekends (from bed, dependent on the time I wake up).
But yeah, it's dependent on the device actually properly powering up rather than just going into standby.
[pinkybrain] don't coffee machine still need to press start/power button, add water and add coffee?
what machine model you got?
[axyh] I've never used a machine that needs that.
I don't use a timer, but if I did, my Rancilio Silvia would turn on and warm up to temperature provided I left the master switch on.
Coffee gets ground by the grinder (which doesn't need to heat up) and water comes in from the reservoir (which just gets filled when it's running low).
[ESEMCE] My Silvia has a power switch, so we turn that on before going to bed (but power from the wall is off).
Smart switch is timer set to turn on at 6:45, I get out of bed at 7am, get dressed, walk up to pre-heated machine, grind, tamp and 30 seconds later have coffee that I deliver to my wife who is still in bed.
[ganjadeals] @ESEMCE: For a moment I thought you managed to make your Nissan Silvia smart.
[strazto] They have other features and not every device requires this
[axyh] I've used them for:
- Turning off indoor cameras (at the wall) when I get home, then turning them back on when I leave (I don't trust the "privacy mode" in cameras - I want the power cut).
- Turning on and off lamps while I'm away (to pretend I'm home).
- Turning on and off set-top boxes that use too much idle power (via an integration with my Logitech remote and Home Assistant).
- Turning on and off Christmas lights (outside and on the tree).
- Reacting to camera motion detection and turning on garden lighting to scare critters away.
And a whole bunch of other things. Mostly via Home Assistant integrations.
[CRIPSYPANCETTA] Well some do some don’t. I use them for
- dehumidifier, that will return to its prior state when turned on
- pool pump (probably biggest automation)
- minor things like outdoor lights, spare bathroom heated towel rail
- ventilation fan under the house, it makes a bit of noise in my bedroom so it’s times during the day
Since I’m using home assistant, have solar, battery, free grid, I can get a bit clever with the pool pump, running more in summer and on sunny days based on battery charge and solar production.
[belongsinforums] You can charge or run things overnight during cheap hours. Also useful for charging scooter batteries on a timer to reduce risk of overcharging
[cheekymonkey97] Thanks for the input all, sounds like it’s not for me. Can’t think of a single use in my life haha
[SoTightEcoLPI] Can these be used with double power point. Ie that and usable next to it
[superforever] https://www.kmart.com.au/product/mirabella-genio-wi-fi-power…
Mirabella Genio Wi-Fi Powerboard 4 Outlets 2 USB-A, 1 USB-C $39 not as cheap as last BIGW deal.
https://www.ozbargain.com.au/node/951130
[strazto] I wouldn't say this is equivalent, although for some uses it might be cost effective
[strazto] Oh cmon, not even 2 hours since you posted it and they're unavailable online lol
[escusemay] Waiting for it to be $9.95 again like last time. Great plugs so far.
[ihadadollar] I wish they made a more compact/cube version. For wall outlets it's fine but for grid style outlets these cover up the row above for my set up :(.
[superforever] Yes, I have these and I also bought this Mirabella Genio powerboard $15 from last deal.
https://www.ozbargain.com.au/comment/17705834/redir
[neonlight] I don't need 4. 4 is too many and also unlucky number
